title: Excel: Intermediate Formulas
subtitle: Calculate Smarter, Not Harder!
overview: This session takes students beyond basic SUM and AVERAGE into the world of Intermediate Excel Formulas. Students learn powerful functions like IF, COUNTIF, SUMIF, VLOOKUP, and nested formulas using real-life data sets like class marks, shop inventories, and cricket scoreboards. By the end, students can make Excel think and decide — not just calculate!

keyTopics: Quick Recap — SUM, AVERAGE, MIN, MAX basics
IF Formula — Make Excel decide: =IF(condition, value_if_true, value_if_false)
Nested IF — Multiple conditions: Grade A / B / C / Fail
COUNTIF — Count cells matching a condition: =COUNTIF(range, criteria)
SUMIF — Add values that meet a condition: =SUMIF(range, criteria, sum_range)
VLOOKUP — Search a table and return a value: =VLOOKUP(lookup_value, table, col_index, FALSE)
Absolute Cell References — Using $ to lock cells in formulas: =$A$1
Real-world Use — Marksheets, shop inventory, cricket scoreboard, attendance tracker

resources.title: Microsoft Excel Online — Free in Browser

activity.title: Build a Smart Class Marksheet with IF, COUNTIF & VLOOKUP
parts: Computer or laptop with MS Excel or Excel Online
Sample class marks dataset (provided by teacher — 10 students, 3 subjects)
Grade chart handout (A: 90+, B: 75–89, C: 60–74, Fail: below 60)
steps: Open Excel and enter the sample dataset: Student Name, Maths, Science, English marks in columns A–D
In column E, calculate Total using: =SUM(B2:D2) and fill down for all students
In column F, calculate Average using: =AVERAGE(B2:D2) and fill down
In column G, use IF formula to show Pass/Fail: =IF(F2>=60,"Pass","Fail")
In column H, use Nested IF to assign Grade: =IF(F2>=90,"A",IF(F2>=75,"B",IF(F2>=60,"C","Fail")))
Below the table, use COUNTIF to count how many students got Grade A: =COUNTIF(H2:H11,"A")
Use SUMIF to find total marks of only students who Passed: =SUMIF(G2:G11,"Pass",E2:E11)
Create a Grade Table on a separate area (columns J–K) with grades and remarks
Use VLOOKUP to auto-fill remarks for each student based on their grade: =VLOOKUP(H2,$J$2:$K$5,2,FALSE)
Challenge: Add conditional formatting — highlight Fail cells in red, Grade A in green
Groups present their marksheet and explain each formula used

quiz.q: What does the IF formula do in Excel?
options: Adds all numbers in a range
Checks a condition and returns one value if TRUE, another if FALSE
quiz.explanation: =IF(condition, value_if_true, value_if_false) — Excel checks the condition and decides which value to show!

quiz.q: Which formula checks if a student's marks (in F2) are above 60 and shows Pass or Fail?
quiz.explanation: =IF(F2>=60,"Pass","Fail") checks if marks are 60 or above — TRUE shows Pass, FALSE shows Fail. Classic IF usage!

quiz.q: What is a Nested IF formula?
options: An IF formula inside a SUM formula
Multiple IF formulas inside each other to handle more than two outcomes
An IF formula that uses VLOOKUP
An IF formula that counts cells
quiz.explanation: Nested IF puts one IF inside another: =IF(score>=90,"A",IF(score>=75,"B",IF(score>=60,"C","Fail"))) — 4 outcomes!

options: Counts cells that match a specific condition or value
Searches a table for a value
quiz.explanation: =COUNTIF(range, criteria) counts how many cells match — e.g. =COUNTIF(G2:G11,"Pass") counts all Pass results!

quiz.q: Which formula counts how many students scored above 80 in the range B2:B11?
quiz.explanation: =COUNTIF(B2:B11,">80") counts every cell in that range where the value is greater than 80. Quick and powerful!

options: Adds only the values in a range that meet a given condition
Counts values that meet a condition
quiz.explanation: =SUMIF(range, criteria, sum_range) adds only values where the condition is met — e.g. total sales only for Mango!

options: Vertical Lookup — searches vertically down a column to find a matching value
quiz.explanation: VLOOKUP = Vertical Lookup! It searches DOWN a column for a match and returns data from another column in the same row.

quiz.q: In =VLOOKUP(H2, $J$2:$K$5, 2, FALSE), what does the number 2 mean?
options: Return the value from the 2nd column of the lookup table
quiz.explanation: The 2 is the column index — return the value from the 2nd column of the table range ($J$2:$K$5). Column 1 = J, Column 2 = K.

quiz.q: What does FALSE mean at the end of a VLOOKUP formula?
options: Look for an exact match only
quiz.explanation: FALSE = Exact Match. Always use FALSE for student names, IDs, or codes. TRUE = approximate match (used for ranges).

quiz.q: What does the $ symbol do in a cell reference like $A$1?
options: Locks the cell reference so it does not change when the formula is copied
quiz.explanation: $ creates an Absolute Reference. $A$1 stays locked when you copy the formula — essential for lookup tables and grade charts!

quiz.q: A teacher wants to add total marks ONLY for students who Passed. Which formula should she use?
quiz.explanation: =SUMIF(G2:G11,"Pass",E2:E11) checks the Pass/Fail column and adds totals only for rows that say Pass. Perfect!

quiz.q: What is the correct syntax for an IF formula?
quiz.explanation: =IF(condition, value_if_true, value_if_false) — always 3 parts: the test, what to show if YES, what to show if NO.

quiz.q: A student writes =COUNTIF(H2:H11,"A"). What will this formula return?
options: The sum of all values in H2:H11
The number of cells in H2:H11 that contain the letter A
The position of A in the column
quiz.explanation: COUNTIF counts matching cells — this counts how many students received a Grade A. Great for class performance reports!

quiz.q: Which of these is a real-world use of intermediate Excel formulas?
options: Building a shop inventory that auto-calculates stock value by category
quiz.explanation: SUMIF, COUNTIF, and VLOOKUP are used daily in shops, hospitals, schools, and offices to analyse real data automatically!

quiz.q: What did students build in the activity 'Smart Class Marksheet'?
options: An Excel spreadsheet using IF, Nested IF, COUNTIF, SUMIF, and VLOOKUP to automate a class results sheet
quiz.explanation: Students built a full automated marksheet — formulas calculated totals, assigned grades, counted results, and looked up remarks!
